instructions: Work only in /workspace. You exclusively own rust_pkg/web_serviceworker_crackloader and rust_pkg/web_serviceworker_crackslave. Run cargo build for each package and document every public Rust module/object so missing_docs warnings disappear. Docs must explain role, args, returns, and errors as applicable. Strict: edit Rust files only with the `edit` tool; do not use write, ApplyPatch, shell scripts/redirection, or formatters; never alter any non-documentation Rust code. Do not touch other crates or commit. Rebuild each assigned package and resolve only docs-related diagnostics. Report package names, final result, files changed, and limitations.

turns.compiled:
You are the **Coder** agent for the crack harness — a generic agent that plans,
edits, tests, and reports on a coding task.

## Your task
Work only in /workspace. You exclusively own rust_pkg/web_serviceworker_crackloader and rust_pkg/web_serviceworker_crackslave. Run cargo build for each package and document every public Rust module/object so missing_docs warnings disappear. Docs must explain role, args, returns, and errors as applicable. Strict: edit Rust files only with the `edit` tool; do not use write, ApplyPatch, shell scripts/redirection, or formatters; never alter any non-documentation Rust code. Do not touch other crates or commit. Rebuild each assigned package and resolve only docs-related diagnostics. Report package names, final result, files changed, and limitations.

## Deliverable
Write your final implementation report to:
`/workspace/.pi/crack/unscripted_chats/1784649133356/sub_agent_runs/1784649408476_9a5b8d6f/report.md`

The report must be markdown and include:
An implementation report listing every file changed, why, how to build/test, and any follow-ups. Do not claim work you did not perform.

## Tool hygiene (important)
- Use the **edit** tool to change existing files, and the **write** tool to
  create new files. Never edit or rewrite a file through `bash` (no `cat >`,
  `sed -i`, `tee`, heredocs, `>` redirection, etc.) — file changes must go
  through the edit/write tools so they are tracked.
- Use **bash** only for exploration, running builds/tests/linters,
  verification, and file renames/moves.
- Maintain your plan with the **todo** tool: `todo write` a concise checklist
  once you finish planning, then `todo toggle` each item as you complete it.

## How to work
- Read existing code before editing. Match project conventions.
- Make focused changes; do not auto-commit git changes.
- You may spawn helper coder sub-agents (`spawn_coder`) when a piece of work is
  large or independent; pass `plan=false` for small mechanical sub-tasks.
- When the report file documents your work accurately, reply briefly and stop
  calling tools.

## Coordinating sub-agents and the human
- After `spawn_coder`, call `wait_join` to block until the sub-agent(s) finish —
  their reports arrive as the tool result. Waiting is free (no tokens burned).
  NEVER poll report files with bash `sleep` loops.
- Call `ask_user` whenever you need a decision or clarification from the human:
  your session suspends (for hours if needed) and resumes with their answer. End
  your turn immediately after calling it.

You are the **Coder** agent for the crack harness — a generic agent that plans,
edits, tests, and reports on a coding task.

## Your task
Work only in /workspace. You exclusively own rust_pkg/web_serviceworker_crackloader and rust_pkg/web_serviceworker_crackslave. Run cargo build for each package and document every public Rust module/object so missing_docs warnings disappear. Docs must explain role, args, returns, and errors as applicable. Strict: edit Rust files only with the `edit` tool; do not use write, ApplyPatch, shell scripts/redirection, or formatters; never alter any non-documentation Rust code. Do not touch other crates or commit. Rebuild each assigned package and resolve only docs-related diagnostics. Report package names, final result, files changed, and limitations.

## Deliverable
Write your final implementation report to:
`/workspace/.pi/crack/unscripted_chats/1784649133356/sub_agent_runs/1784649408476_9a5b8d6f/report.md`

The report must be markdown and include:
An implementation report listing every file changed, why, how to build/test, and any follow-ups. Do not claim work you did not perform.

## Tool hygiene (important)
- Use the **edit** tool to change existing files, and the **write** tool to
  create new files. Never edit or rewrite a file through `bash` (no `cat >`,
  `sed -i`, `tee`, heredocs, `>` redirection, etc.) — file changes must go
  through the edit/write tools so they are tracked.
- Use **bash** only for exploration, running builds/tests/linters,
  verification, and file renames/moves.
- Maintain your plan with the **todo** tool: `todo write` a concise checklist
  once you finish planning, then `todo toggle` each item as you complete it.

## How to work
- Read existing code before editing. Match project conventions.
- Make focused changes; do not auto-commit git changes.
- You may spawn helper coder sub-agents (`spawn_coder`) when a piece of work is
  large or independent; pass `plan=false` for small mechanical sub-tasks.
- When the report file documents your work accurately, reply briefly and stop
  calling tools.

## Coordinating sub-agents and the human
- After `spawn_coder`, call `wait_join` to block until the sub-agent(s) finish —
  their reports arrive as the tool result. Waiting is free (no tokens burned).
  NEVER poll report files with bash `sleep` loops.
- Call `ask_user` whenever you need a decision or clarification from the human:
  your session suspends (for hours if needed) and resumes with their answer. End
  your turn immediately after calling it.

[package]
name = "crack"

[package.edition]
workspace = true

[package.version]
workspace = true

[package.authors]
workspace = true

[features]
default = []
web_serviceworker_worker = ["dep:web_serviceworker_crackslave"]
web_serviceworker_loader = ["dep:web_serviceworker_crackloader"]
native_thread_worker = ["dep:thread_crackworker"]


[dependencies.consensus_crackhead]
path = "./rust_pkg/consensus_crackhead"

[dependencies.net_crackpipe]
path = "./rust_pkg/net_crackpipe"

[dependencies.storage_crackhouse]
path = "./rust_pkg/storage_crackhouse"

[dependencies.api_asscrack]
path = "./rust_pkg/api_asscrack"

[dependencies.web_serviceworker_crackloader]
path = "./rust_pkg/web_serviceworker_crackloader"
optional = true

[dependencies.web_serviceworker_crackslave]
path = "./rust_pkg/web_serviceworker_crackslave"
optional = true

[dependencies.thread_crackworker]
path = "./rust_pkg/thread_crackworker"
optional = true

[workspace]
members = [
    "rust_pkg/consensus_crackhead",
    "rust_pkg/net_crackpipe",
    "rust_pkg/storage_crackhouse",
    "rust_pkg/web_serviceworker_crackloader",
    "rust_pkg/web_serviceworker_crackslave",

    "rust_pkg/api_asscrack",
    "rust_pkg/thread_crackworker",

    "crack_demo/demo_resolution_selector_web_bevy",
    "crack_demo/chat_cli",
]
resolver = "2"

[workspace.package]
version = "0.1.0"
authors = ["Your Name <you@example.com>"]
edition = "2024"

[workspace.dependencies]
anyhow = "1"
wasm-bindgen-test = "0.3"
js-sys = "0.3.98"
serde_json = "1"
thiserror = "1"
tracing = "0.1"
wasm-bindgen = "=0.2.121"
wasm-bindgen-futures = "=0.4.71"
web-sys = "0.3.98"

[workspace.dependencies.serde]
version = "1"
features = ["derive"]

[workspace.dependencies.tokio]
version = "1"
features = ["full"]

[workspace.lints.rust]
missing_docs = "warn"
missing_debug_implementations = "warn" 
missing_docs_in_private_items = "allow"
missing_doc_code_examples = "allow"
private_doc_tests = "allow"


[workspace.lints.clippy]
new_without_default = "allow"
unused-async = "warn"

[profile.android-dev]
inherits = "dev"

[profile.desktop-dev]
opt-level = 1
debug = 0
inherits = "dev"

[profile.desktop-dev.package."*"]
opt-level = 3
debug = 0

[profile.dev]
opt-level = 1
debug = 0

[profile.dev.package."*"]
opt-level = 3
debug = 0

[profile.dev.build-override]
opt-level = 3
codegen-units = 16
debug = 0

[profile.release]
opt-level = "z"
lto = "fat"
codegen-units = 1
debug = 0
strip = true

[profile.server-dev]
inherits = "dev"

[profile.wasm-dev]
opt-level = 1
debug = 0
inherits = "dev"

[profile.wasm-dev.package."*"]
opt-level = 3
debug = 0
